agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The system must enforce medical licensing compliance, scheduling integrity, access control over sensitive medical records, and auditability of consultation notes through time-based locking.","bounded_context":"Appointment & Consultation Management — covers physician licensing verification, appointment scheduling, cancellation policy enforcement, medical record access control, and consultation note lifecycle.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Patient","definition":"An individual registered on the platform who can book appointments, view their own medical records, and cancel appointments subject to policy constraints."},{"term":"Physician","definition":"A licensed medical professional who provides consultations. Must have a verified license before accepting any bookings."},{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled consultation between exactly one patient and one physician, defined by a start time and end time. Cannot overlap with another appointment for the same physician."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"An internal platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments inside the 2-hour cancellation window."},{"term":"License Number","definition":"A physician's medical license identifier that must be verified against an authoritative source before the physician is eligible to accept bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling violation where two or more appointments for the same physician have overlapping time ranges. This must never occur."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The 2-hour period before an appointment's start time. Before this window, the patient may self-cancel. Inside this window, only a Staff Administrator can cancel."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after a consultation. Becomes read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"Medical Records","definition":"Patient health information accessible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician for a given appointment."},{"term":"Note Locking","definition":"The irreversible transition of consultation notes from editable to read-only state, triggered automatically 48 hours after the appointment's end time."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient can only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unlicensed physicians to see patients is a patient safety and legal compliance violation. The system is in a corrupt state if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"Given a physician whose license_verified flag is false, any attempt to create an appointment with that physician MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments with overlapping time ranges.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking a physician is a physical constraint — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. Data integrity is compromised if overlapping appointments exist.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two appointments may exist where appointment_A.start < appointment_B.end AND appointment_B.start < appointment_A.end."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is more than 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., to 4 hours or 1 hour) by management without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request MUST be rejected when now >= (appointment.start_time - 2 hours)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A Staff Administrator can cancel any appointment regardless of the cancellation window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an access-control policy granting override privileges. The specific role authorized could change without system corruption.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a Staff Administrator MUST succeed even when now >= (appointment.start_time - 2 hours)."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and the treating physician for a given appointment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Unauthorized access to medical records is a HIPAA/privacy violation. If any other actor can view records, the system is in a compliance-critical failure state.","testable_assertion":"An access request for patient medical records MUST return 403/denied unless the requester is the patient OR a physician with a confirmed appointment with that patient."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Consultation notes become locked (read-only) 48 hours after the appointment end time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 48-hour window is a clinical documentation policy. It could be changed to 24 or 72 hours without corrupting the system. However, the locking mechanism itself is important for auditability.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to edit consultation notes when now >= (appointment.end_time + 48 hours) MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before they are eligible to accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a regulatory gate. A physician without license verification is not legally allowed to practice through the platform. Bypassing this corrupts the compliance state.","testable_assertion":"A physician record without license_verified = true MUST NOT appear in available booking slots and MUST NOT be assignable to new appointments."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The system rejects the booking with an error indicating the physician is not available for appointments"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician who has a verified license and an existing appointment from 10:00-11:00","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with the same physician from 10:30-11:30","then":"The system rejects the booking with an error indicating a scheduling conflict"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician with a verified license and no conflicting appointments","when":"A patient books an appointment with that physician","then":"The appointment is created successfully and the physician's schedule is updated"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A patient with an appointment starting in 3 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient with an appointment starting in 1 hour","when":"The patient requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and informs the patient that only a Staff Administrator can cancel within 2 hours of the start time"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An appointment starting in 30 minutes","when":"A Staff Administrator requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A patient with medical records and a treating physician for an active appointment","when":"The treating physician requests access to that patient's medical records","then":"The system grants access and displays the records"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient with medical records","when":"A physician who does NOT have an appointment with that patient requests access to their records","then":"The system denies access with a forbidden error"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A completed appointment that ended 47 hours ago","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A completed appointment that ended 49 hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The system rejects the edit and indicates the notes are locked"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A patient viewing their own medical records","when":"The patient requests access","then":"The system grants access and displays the records"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A physician with a verified license and an existing appointment from 10:00-11:00","when":"A patient books an appointment with that physician from 11:00-12:00 (adjacent, non-overlapping)","then":"The appointment is created successfully — adjacency is not overlap"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Appointment booked exactly 2 hours before start time — patient cancellation boundary","expected_behavior":"The request is at the boundary (now == start_time - 2 hours). Per the rule 'up to 2 hours before,' this means strictly more than 2 hours. The cancellation should be REJECTED because 'up to 2 hours' means the window has closed at exactly 2 hours. The patient must contact a Staff Administrator."},{"scenario":"Physician license is revoked AFTER appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing appointments should be flagged for review or cancellation by a Staff Administrator. The physician must be immediately removed from future booking availability. The system should not allow the appointment to proceed with an unverified physician."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes edited at exactly the 48-hour mark after appointment end","expected_behavior":"At exactly 48 hours (now == end_time + 48h), the notes should be locked. The boundary condition means edits at or after the 48-hour mark are rejected."},{"scenario":"Two concurrent booking requests for the same physician in the same time slot (race condition)","expected_behavior":"Only one booking succeeds. The system must use optimistic locking, database-level constraints, or serialized transactions to prevent double-booking. The second request receives a scheduling conflict error."},{"scenario":"Patient attempts to view another patient's medical records","expected_behavior":"Access is denied. A patient can only view their own records."},{"scenario":"Staff Administrator attempts to view patient medical records","expected_behavior":"The rules state records are visible ONLY to the patient and treating physician. A Staff Administrator without a treatment relationship should NOT have access to medical records, even though they have cancellation privileges."},{"scenario":"Appointment is cancelled after consultation notes have been written but before the 48-hour lock","expected_behavior":"Notes should still follow the locking lifecycle based on the original appointment end time. Cancellation does not destroy or prematurely lock notes — they remain editable until 48 hours after the original end time."},{"scenario":"Physician has back-to-back appointments with zero gap","expected_behavior":"This is allowed. Appointments [10:00-11:00] and [11:00-12:00] do not overlap because one ends exactly when the other starts. Overlap requires start_A < end_B AND start_B < end_A (strict inequality)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect scheduling (double-booking) leads to cancelled consultations, refunds, and patient churn. License verification failures expose the platform to malpractice lawsuits and regulatory fines. Consultation note integrity is critical for medical-legal defensibility.","operational":"Scheduling conflict detection must be performant under concurrent load. License verification is a prerequisite gate in the physician onboarding flow. The 48-hour note locking mechanism requires a reliable background job or event-driven trigger. Cancellation policy enforcement requires accurate server-side time handling.","user":"Patients expect seamless booking without conflicts. The cancellation window policy must be clearly communicated in the UI to avoid frustration. Physicians need timely reminders to finalize consultation notes before the 48-hour lock. Record access restrictions build patient trust.","risk":"HIPAA/privacy violations from unauthorized medical record access carry severe regulatory penalties. Unlicensed physician consultations represent existential legal risk. Double-booking degrades service quality and physician trust. Note locking failures compromise audit trails for malpractice defense.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Booking reliability, cancellation flexibility, privacy of medical records, trust in physician credentials"},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Schedule integrity (no double-bookings), clear note-editing deadlines, access scoped to their own patients only"},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Override cancellation responsibilities, potential incident handling for license revocations, no default medical record access"},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"License verification audit trail, HIPAA-compliant access controls, locked consultation notes for legal defensibility"},{"group":"Platform Operators","impact":"Concurrency handling for booking, background job reliability for note locking, uptime of license verification integration"}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear and internally consistent. All six rules are well-defined with explicit actors, actions, and constraints. No contradictions exist between rules. Minor boundary conditions (exact 2-hour mark, exact 48-hour mark) require convention choices but are standard edge cases, not ambiguities. The only areas requiring assumptions are the overlap definition (strict vs. inclusive boundaries) and what happens to downstream data when a physician's license is revoked post-booking.","high_confidence_areas":["Double-booking prevention rule is unambiguous","License verification as a prerequisite for booking is clear","Cancellation policy roles (patient vs. Staff Administrator) are well-defined","Medical record access control scoping is explicit","Consultation note locking trigger and timing are clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'up to 2 hours' is strictly greater than or greater-than-or-equal — convention assumed as strictly greater than 2 hours","Behavior when physician license is revoked after bookings exist — not addressed in spec","Whether Staff Administrators can access medical records — spec implies no, but operational reality may require it","Whether cancelled appointments still trigger the 48-hour note lock or if notes are handled differently","Definition of 'treating physician' — scoped per appointment or across all historical appointments with that patient"],"assumptions":["Overlap is defined using strict inequality: A.start < B.end AND B.start < A.end, meaning adjacent appointments are allowed","'Up to 2 hours before' means the patient can cancel when time remaining is strictly greater than 2 hours","Consultation note locking is based on the original appointment end time regardless of cancellation","'Treating physician' means the physician assigned to a specific appointment, not all physicians who have ever treated the patient","License verification is a binary state (verified/not verified) checked at booking time"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

It could be changed to 24 or 72 hours without corrupting the system.
